scope_assessment.rationale: The plan introduces one new module (quality.ts) and makes minimal, additive changes to five existing files. All modifications use optional parameters with defaults, preserving backward compatibility. The new module is a pure function with no side effects, fitting cleanly into the existing synchronous initialization pattern.

files_to_create.src/visual/quality.ts: New pure-function module. No coupling risk. Depends only on the BrowserSignals interface which is stable.
files_to_create.tests/visual/quality.test.ts: Standard test file following existing test conventions.

files_to_modify.src/input/signals.ts: Adding one optional field (deviceMemory) to BrowserSignals interface. Non-breaking change; all existing consumers ignore unknown fields.
files_to_modify.src/visual/scene.ts: Adding optional resolutionScale parameter to initScene(). Default 1.0 preserves current behavior. Canvas sizing logic is already in this file.
files_to_modify.src/visual/resize.ts: Adding optional resolutionScale parameter to attachResizeHandler(). Minimal change, default preserves current behavior.
files_to_modify.src/visual/systems/particleField.ts: Replacing hardcoded MAX_PARTICLES=600 with configurable cap and adding sparkle toggle. The reviewer correction about preserving the density/structureComplexity formula is critical and correctly noted.
files_to_modify.src/main.ts: Wiring quality profile into existing synchronous init block. computeQuality(readSignals()) runs before createParticleField() which is correct since both are synchronous. No change to async initialization order.

files_correctly_excluded.src/visual/renderLoop.ts: Reviewer correctly identified this file needs no changes. The render loop delegates drawing to the geometry system; quality scaling flows through particleField.ts.

dependency_verification.US-004: signals.ts exists with BrowserSignals interface — verified
dependency_verification.US-009: particleField.ts exists with MAX_PARTICLES=600 and sparkle/jitter logic — verified

architectural_alignment.privacy_model: No impact. Quality detection uses only in-memory signals already collected by readSignals(). No new data collection or persistence.
architectural_alignment.initialization_pattern: Aligns with existing sync-first pattern. computeQuality() is pure and synchronous, fitting before createParticleField() in the init sequence.
architectural_alignment.graceful_degradation: Consistent with project philosophy. Missing deviceMemory (Safari/Firefox) falls back to null; scoring handles nulls conservatively. Default tier is 'medium' which is safe.
architectural_alignment.mapping_philosophy: Quality scaling adjusts quantity (particle count) and resolution, not the visual character (palette, motion, evolution). This preserves the partially-legible mapping identity.

reviewer_corrections_assessment.renderLoop_exclusion: Valid. renderLoop.ts has no direct particle or resolution logic.
reviewer_corrections_assessment.density_formula_preservation: Critical and valid. The formula `Math.floor(params.density * MAX_PARTICLES) * (0.6 + structureComplexity * 0.4)` must use config.maxParticles as the cap, not bypass density/complexity scaling.
reviewer_corrections_assessment.init_ordering: Valid. The synchronous ordering (readSignals → computeQuality → createParticleField → startLoop) must be explicit since all are synchronous and main.ts currently creates the particle field before async work begins.

risks.mitigation: Plan handles null gracefully. Conservative default tier (medium) prevents over-penalizing Safari/Firefox users.

risks.risk: Scoring heuristic may misclassify some devices
risks.mitigation: Medium tier is the safe default. The weighted scoring approach with multiple signals reduces single-signal misclassification. Can be tuned post-deployment.

risks.risk: High-DPR small screens (iPhone SE) could be scored too high
risks.mitigation: Plan includes screen pixel count (0.15 weight) and touch-capable penalty (0.15 weight) plus explicit low-core-count penalty for touch devices.

adr_recommendation.rationale: This is a tactical feature addition, not an architectural decision. The quality tier system is a straightforward capability-detection pattern with no novel trade-offs. No existing ADRs would be affected.

test_strategy_assessment: Adequate. Unit tests cover device archetypes for tier classification. Integration coverage via particleField tests validates maxParticles cap. Existing 29-file test suite covers regression surface for signals, scene, mappings, and audio.
